title: tulipa neglecta subsp. atroguttata
intro: Tulipa neglecta subsp. atroguttata is a lesser-known species of tulip, belonging to the Liliaceae family, characterized by its dark leaves and spotted or striped flowers. The medicinal properties of Tulipa neglecta subsp. atroguttata include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits associated with this plant include its possible use in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Tulipa neglecta subsp. atroguttata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
